insights.root_cause: Skills are written around repository conventions (plan template, SKILL.md location, PR comment extraction) rather than discovering arbitrary layouts.
insights.solution: Any edits or invocations must respect the repository conventions and naming patterns; callers that move files or change layout must also be updated to maintain compatibility.

insights.title: GitHub API access and potential sensitive outputs
insights.problem: Scripts fetch release/pr data via gh api and may surface metadata; they require authenticated access and can expose content externally if used carelessly.
insights.root_cause: Integration with GitHub is done through the gh CLI and relies on authenticated sessions or CI tokens.
insights.solution: Operate these scripts only in environments with appropriate credentials and audit what is printed or stored; maintain awareness of token scopes and network access when executing or modifying these scripts.

insights.title: Reasoning tests depend on provider-specific serialization and runtime binary
insights.problem: test-reasoning.sh runs provider-integrated tests and asserts structured outputs; missing providers, different provider schemas, or incorrect BINARY will cause failures.
insights.root_cause: The script encodes expectations about JSON output shapes and provider behavior.
insights.solution: When running or changing these tests, ensure provider configuration and the BINARY under test match the expectations; treat failures as environment/test-config mismatches unless intentionally changing test intent.

key_files.why: Primary per-plan validator enforcing filename, markdown structure, checkbox usage and task-quality heuristics — central to plan quality gating.
Bulk runner that iterates plan files and summarizes pass/fail counts; used to produce repository-wide validation reports (and likely CI summaries).
Fetches release and PR metadata from GitHub using gh and jq — upstream data source for release-note assembly and validation.
Structured smoke/regression test harness for reasoning/serialization across providers; exercises the CLI/BINARY and asserts JSON outputs.
Canonical plan template used by validators and authors as a format reference — informs the validators' expectations about required sections.

construct_relationships: validate-all-plans.sh invokes validate-plan.sh for each plan file and aggregates their exit codes and summaries.

SKILL.md documents map to adjacent scripts: each SKILL.md provides human-readable intent and usage for the scripts in the same skill folder (e.g., create-github-issue SKILL.md for the gh-based issue helper).
fetch-release-data.sh supplies structured release/pr metadata that validate-release-notes.sh consumes to check length and content constraints.

test-reasoning.sh depends on a configurable BINARY (the CLI under test) and uses helper functions (json_get, assert_field) to validate provider outputs; those helpers coordinate parsing and test lifecycle.
Conflict-resolve and validate-conflicts scripts interact with git state and filesystem layout to detect unresolved markers, producing artifacts or exit codes used by higher-level workflows.
notable_relationships: These scripts are intended to run both locally and in CI; CI jobs that gate merges may call validate-all-plans.sh, validate-conflicts.sh, and validate-release-notes.sh, so script behavior influences mergeability.
Multiple scripts invoke external CLIs (gh, jq, git). Changes to the environment, CLI versions, or token availability upstream will affect script outcomes.
SKILL.md content serves as documentation for tooling that may be surfaced to agents or humans; updating scripts without aligning SKILL.md can create mismatches between documentation and behavior.

Provider-dependent tests (test-reasoning) and release data fetchers require network access and credentials supplied by the runtime environment (local dev or CI) — external systems are upstream dependencies.
recommendations: Preserve existing exit codes and summary text formats: callers and CI rely on precise pass/fail signaling and summaries.

When running or updating scripts verify presence and versions of external tools (gh, jq, git) and required environment variables (BINARY, VERSION, REPO, MAX_CHARS) in the target environment (local shell vs CI).
Do not assume interactive authentication: ensure gh API calls are executed in an environment with non-interactive credentials (CI token or pre-authenticated gh) to reproduce CI behavior.

Respect repository layout and naming conventions used by validators (plans directory, plan filename/date patterns, SKILL.md locations) — moving files will require coordinated updates to callers and documentation.

Run the relevant smoke/validation scripts after edits in the same execution context as CI (non-interactive shell with required tools and creds) to detect regressions that affect gating or provider tests.

crates.description: The crates/ folder is the workspace root for Forge's Rust crates: it contains the domain model, configuration, application orchestration, API adapter, services, and infra/CLI entrypoints. Together these crates implement the end-to-end agent/chat flows, tool dispatch/execution, provider/model resolution and persistence that the CLI, TUI and remote callers depend on. Changes here affect runtime behavior, provider selection, conversation persistence, streaming semantics and cross-cutting invariants (config, metrics, truncation, security).
key_behaviors: Trait-driven decoupling: high-level async traits (API, Services, Infra) separate platform-specific implementations from application logic so callers (CLI, TUI, tests, RPC) use stable interfaces.

End-to-end chat orchestration: building context, resolving per-agent providers/models/tools, invoking provider chat APIs, applying transformers, running tool calls (parallel Task vs sequential system tools), streaming lifecycle events and responses.

Tool dispatch and execution pipeline: ToolRegistry validates/dispatches calls, ToolExecutor normalizes paths/args and enforces read-before-edit policies, then ToolOperation converts results into UI-facing outputs with truncation and metrics bookkeeping.

Configuration and provider resolution: layered ForgeConfig merged from defaults/legacy/global/env drives provider selection, per-agent overrides, and operational limits; config updates must be visible to services and API adapters at runtime.
Persistence & indexing: conversations and snapshots are persisted via repo layer; workspace indexing and semantic search interact with external servers (gRPC/HTTP) and may stream content externally.

Streaming and concurrency concerns: chat and tool outputs are streamed asynchronously; ordering, interleaving and lifecycle event notifications are critical to correct UI rendering and tool stdout behavior.

insights.title: Avoid stale in-memory config copies
insights.problem: API or services held a single cached ForgeConfig copy so after config updates the runtime used stale configuration causing inconsistent provider/agent resolution and retries.
insights.root_cause: Config was captured at construction time and not re-read or updated in all code paths.
insights.solution: Read or inject the canonical config at operation time (EnvironmentInfra.get_config or explicit update_config API) so runtime components always operate with current ForgeConfig.

insights.title: Per-agent provider/model semantics
insights.problem: Many flows assumed a single global provider/model; agent-specific provider/model selection and commit/suggest config were missing or ambiguous.
insights.root_cause: API and application flows were designed around a global active provider/model and ambiguous method names.
insights.solution: Expose per-agent provider/model endpoints and include commit/suggest config across provider models; ensure chat and generator flows accept AgentId and resolve provider/model per-agent.

insights.title: Preserve usage metrics and reasoning continuity during compaction
insights.problem: Compaction replaced message sequences with summaries but lost accumulated usage metrics and sometimes broke reasoning continuity or duplicated reasoning_details across compactions.
insights.root_cause: Compactor discarded or failed to transfer usage/last-reasoning when summarizing multiple messages into one.
insights.solution: Accumulate and transfer usage metadata into the summary entry and inject the last non-empty reasoning_details into the preserved assistant message while avoiding exponential accumulation of reasoning across repeated compactions.

insights.title: Normalize paths and report true file metadata
insights.problem: Relative paths and truncated content led to mismatched operations and incorrect total_lines/metadata reported to the UI and metrics.
insights.root_cause: Tools accepted relative paths and reported counts from truncated slices rather than whole-file metadata; multiple places duplicated path assembly logic.
insights.solution: Normalize relative file paths to absolute cwd-based paths at the executor entrypoint and report actual file totals (total_lines) from full-file metadata, not from slices returned to UI.

insights.title: Prevent stdout interleaving and ensure lifecycle header visibility
insights.problem: Tool stdout could interleave with UI lifecycle headers because execution started before UI had emitted start events.
insights.root_cause: Orchestrator emitted ToolCallStart then immediately executed the tool without reliable notification/flush to the UI, allowing concurrent stdout to mix.
insights.solution: Notify or yield to the sender/UI before running the tool (ToolCallStart notifier) so headers are rendered and ordering preserved; keep lifecycle events consistent and single-sourced.

insights.title: Tool args must be normalized before persistence or transforms
insights.problem: Tool arguments were persisted or transformed with inconsistent types (e.g., non-stringified args), causing downstream provider transforms to malfunction.
insights.root_cause: ToolCallContext or arguments were not normalized to canonical shapes before passing through transformation pipelines and persistence.
insights.solution: Insert a normalization stage into the orchestrator/transformer pipeline to stringify or canonicalize tool arguments prior to transforms, dispatch and storage.

insights.title: Retry empty/failed LLM-generated commit messages
insights.problem: Commit message generation sometimes returned empty or transiently failed with no retry, resulting in failed commits or poor UX.
insights.root_cause: No retry logic existed around commit message generation and empty results were treated as final.
insights.solution: Retry commit message generation on empty/failed responses with a bounded retry policy and treat empty messages as retryable failures.

insights.title: Infra trait changes require test fixture updates
insights.problem: Changes to infra trait signatures (e.g., EnvironmentInfra.get_config and read_batch_utf8 streaming semantics) broke tests and default fixtures (missing new fields like parallel_file_reads).
insights.root_cause: Trait methods were evolved to expose richer behavior and streaming; existing test fixtures weren't updated to populate new fields or abide by new return shapes.
insights.solution: Update test harness defaults and mocks to provide new fields/behaviors (parallel_file_reads) and adapt test expectations to streaming/batched file read semantics.

key_files.why: Defines the async API trait that callers (CLI/TUI/RPC/tests) use — changes affect external caller contracts, threading and streaming semantics.
Concrete adapter that composes ForgeServices/ForgeRepo/ForgeInfra and implements the API trait — it's the bridge between runtime infra and external callers.
High-level application orchestrator (ForgeApp.chat/compact/list_tools) that composes infra/services and is central to chat flows and per-agent provider/model resolution.
Orchestrator embodies the agent turn loop, lifecycle events and tool execution ordering — critical for streaming behavior, retries, and lifecycle consistency.
Canonical ForgeConfig schema and layered merging logic — changes here cascade to provider selection, timeouts, defaults and merge behavior across the workspace.

construct_relationships: API trait (forge_api/src/api.rs) is implemented by ForgeAPI (forge_api/src/forge_api.rs), which composes ForgeServices, ForgeRepo and ForgeInfra to satisfy callers.
ForgeApp (forge_app/src/app.rs) depends on Services/Infra traits to build chat context, resolve per-agent provider/model/tool definitions, call the Orchestrator, and persist/compact conversations.

Orchestrator (forge_app/src/orch.rs) calls ToolRegistry to validate/resolve tools and then ToolExecutor to execute normalized ToolCatalog inputs; results are wrapped as ToolOperation and serialized into UI outputs via into_tool_output.
Compactor (forge_app/src/compact.rs) consumes conversation message sequences produced by Orchestrator/ForgeApp, produces summary frames and must preserve usage Metrics that ToolOperation accumulates.

ForgeConfig (forge_config/src/config.rs) flows into EnvironmentInfra.get_config and is read by ForgeAPI/ForgeApp/ToolRegistry/GitApp for provider/model defaults, timeouts and commit/suggest configuration.
notable_relationships: forge_main (CLI/TUI) bootstraps ForgeAPI/ForgeApp and maps CLI flags (provider/model/agent) into API initialization; breaking API or provider/model selection semantics impacts CLI behavior directly.

forge_infra provides concrete trait implementations used throughout forge_app and forge_services; changes to infra trait signatures (e.g., get_config, read_batch_utf8) require coordinated updates to services, app, tests and mocks.

forge_repo persists conversations and implements proto/gRPC contracts used by workspace indexing; schema or domain model changes in forge_domain or repo proto types will impact storage, migrations and CI (protoc).

forge_domain types (Provider, ToolCatalog, FileInfo) are widely re-exported and relied on; altering serialization shapes or removing fields (internal IDs) affects persistence, snapshots, formatter outputs and tests.
CI expectations (protoc availability, RUSTFLAGS=-Dwarnings, cargo llvm-cov) mean code changes must compile cleanly with no warnings and preserve proto generation compatibility.
recommendations: Preserve API trait stability: any changes to async trait signatures, streaming return types, Send+Sync guarantees or DTO shapes require updating ForgeAPI and all callers (CLI, tests, RPC adapters).

When touching configuration flows, ensure config updates are visible at operation time (use EnvironmentInfra.get_config or call update_config) to avoid stale behavior; update any cached copies in services/API.
Respect per-agent provider/model semantics: accept AgentId where required and ensure provider/model resolution uses per-agent overrides and commit/suggest config fields.
Keep streaming and lifecycle ordering intact: notify/send lifecycle events (ToolCallStart/End, Request/Response) before executing tools to avoid UI interleaving; preserve event semantics for tests.
Maintain accurate metrics and file metadata: update compaction and ToolOperation logic to preserve accumulated usage, report true total_lines and avoid leaking internal IDs in serialized outputs.
Normalize tool inputs early: canonicalize paths and arguments at ToolExecutor entry so downstream services, persistence and transforms receive consistent shapes.
Update tests and fixtures when traits evolve: adjust orch_setup TestContext, mocks and unit tests to provide new fields (e.g., parallel_file_reads) and new return shapes (streamed batch reads).

Run workspace-level checks after edits: cargo test --workspace, cargo insta test --accept where applicable, and ensure no warnings (CI sets -Dwarnings); if proto/domain types changed, ensure protoc generation matches CI protoc version.

..description: This repository root contains the core Rust workspace implementing Forge's agent/chat orchestration, service/infra abstractions, tool dispatch and formatting layers, plus a small TypeScript benchmarks/eval harness and prompt templates. The Rust crates define async trait surfaces (API/Services/Infra), a concrete API adapter, the ForgeApp orchestration, tool execution pipeline, compaction/formatting and git/commit flows; the TS benchmarks provide an independent runner used for evaluation and CI checks. Changes in this tree affect runtime behavior (provider/model resolution, tool safety policies, truncation/metrics, config semantics) and also the external evaluation tooling and templates that shape LLM interactions.
key_behaviors: Trait-driven layering: API, Services and Infra traits decouple platform specifics from app logic, with concrete adapters (ForgeAPI, ForgeServices, ForgeRepo, ForgeInfra) wiring components together.

Agent/chat orchestration: ForgeApp → Orchestrator run loop builds context, resolves per-agent providers/models, calls provider chat APIs, applies transformers, executes tool calls (parallel task vs sequential system tools), and streams lifecycle events.

Tool dispatch pipeline: ToolExecutor normalizes paths/args, enforces read-before-edit and other policies, invokes lower-level services, converts results into ToolOperation and ToolOutput with truncation and metrics bookkeeping.

Context compaction and formatting: Compactor reduces message sequences into summaries while preserving usage metrics and reasoning continuity; formatters map tool inputs/outputs into UI-friendly ChatResponseContent.

Provider/model resolution and configuration: Many flows resolve provider/model per-agent and must operate against up-to-date ForgeConfig; API and app layers surface provider auth flows and model listing.

Benchmarks/eval flow: TypeScript runner parses task specs, expands CSV/inline sources into contexts, renders templates into shell commands, executes commands with timeouts, streams logs and validates outputs in parallel.

insights.title: Avoid stale in-memory config
insights.problem: API/App layers held a single cached ForgeConfig copy and continued operating against stale configuration after updates, causing inconsistent provider/model resolution and other behavior.
insights.root_cause: Config was captured at construction and not re-read from a single authoritative source (env/infra) when mutations occurred.
insights.solution: Callers and long-lived objects must re-read or obtain config from infra/get_config when performing resolution or after update_config operations; maintain a single source-of-truth and thread config explicitly where required to avoid stale state.

insights.title: Per-agent provider/model resolution
insights.problem: Code paths assumed a single global active provider/model which led to incorrect model/provider being used for sub-agents, suggestions, and lightweight operations.
insights.root_cause: API and app surfaces were designed around a single active provider/model instead of per-agent overrides.
insights.solution: Flows that construct LLM contexts, suggest/generate commands, or generate commit messages must resolve provider/model per-AgentId (or from explicit suggest/commit configs) and support fallbacks, ensuring small, suggest-specific providers are chosen when configured.

insights.title: Preserve usage and reasoning when compacting
insights.problem: Compaction replaced message ranges but dropped or duplicated usage metrics and reasoning_details, corrupting aggregated usage and breaking reasoning continuity.
insights.root_cause: Compactor replaced sequences without transferring accumulated Metrics and last reasoning details properly.
insights.solution: Compaction must aggregate and transfer accumulated usage into the summary message and preserve the last non-empty reasoning_details into the surviving assistant frame; account for metrics when computing totals and when serializing/persisting compacted conversations.

insights.title: Normalize tool arguments and paths consistently
insights.problem: Tool calls and persisted tool-args sometimes contained unnormalized types (non-stringified args) or relative paths, causing provider transforms, service calls, and tests to fail or misbehave.
insights.root_cause: ToolCall contexts and orchestrator pipelines did not consistently normalize/serialize arguments or convert relative to absolute paths before dispatch.
insights.solution: Insert normalization (stringify/structure normalization) into the orchestrator transformer pipeline and ensure ToolExecutor resolves relative paths to absolute based on environment.cwd before calling services; maintain consistent types for persistence and downstream transforms.

insights.title: Accurate reporting and temp-file handling for truncated outputs
insights.problem: Reported totals (e.g., total_lines) were computed from truncated slices rather than actual file totals; truncation handling exposed internal IDs and inconsistent metadata in outputs.
insights.root_cause: into_tool_output and related serialization logic used the returned/truncated slice for reporting and included internal ids/fields instead of FileInfo metadata.
insights.solution: Compute and report canonical totals from full file metadata (FileInfo), attach temp-file paths for full outputs when truncation happens, and avoid exporting internal IDs; ensure metrics and conversation FileOperation entries reflect true totals and content_hashes.

insights.title: Infra method signatures and streaming semantics changed
insights.problem: read_batch_utf8 semantics and other infra surfaces changed to stream individual file results, and EnvironmentInfra gained get_config(), requiring updates to callers and implementations.
insights.root_cause: Evolving needs for better error handling and unified config access led to signature and surface changes.
insights.solution: When editing high-level code, update implementations and tests to match new infra signatures (handle per-file errors and streaming returns) and prefer invoking get_config() for authoritative config; ensure mocks and test fixtures adopt new method shapes.

insights.title: Test fixtures must evolve with new fields and types
insights.problem: Tests and fixtures assumed older shapes (e.g., missing parallel_file_reads or using raw start/end fields instead of FileInfo), causing failing tests after API/domain changes.
insights.root_cause: Refactors introduced new fields and domain types but tests/fixtures were not updated accordingly.
insights.solution: When changing domain shapes or adding fields, update TestContext defaults and formatter fixtures to supply new fields (e.g., parallel_file_reads) and migrate fixtures to use newer structs (FileInfo).

key_files.why: Defines the async API trait that external callers (CLI/TUI/RPC) rely on; changes here affect public surface and RPC compatibility.
High-level application orchestrator that coordinates chat flows, config resolution, compaction and provider/model lookup — central to runtime behavior.
The Orchestrator implements the agent turn loop, lifecycle events, tool call sequencing and transformer pipeline; editing impacts control flow, streaming, and tool execution ordering.
Bridges tool catalog inputs to services; enforces read-before-edit policies, path normalization and truncation temp-file handling — safety-critical for file operations.
Entrypoint for the TypeScript evaluation runner used by CI and manual checks; edits affect evaluation workflows and command generation/validation.

construct_relationships: API trait (crates/forge_api/src/api.rs) is implemented by ForgeAPI (crates/forge_api/src/forge_api.rs) which composes ForgeServices and ForgeRepo and delegates infra operations to ForgeInfra implementations.

ForgeApp (crates/forge_app/src/app.rs) uses Services traits (crates/forge_app/src/services.rs) and Orchestrator (orch.rs) to execute chat flows; it resolves per-agent providers/models and injects system/user prompts from templates.

Orchestrator (orch.rs) invokes ToolExecutor (tool_executor.rs) to run tool calls; ToolExecutor calls lower-level service traits (read/write/patch/shell/fetch) and returns ToolOperation objects which Operation.into_tool_output serializes and updates conversation Metrics.

Compactor (compact.rs) consumes message sequences produced by Orchestrator/ForgeApp and produces summary frames that must preserve usage metrics and reasoning_details for downstream rendering and persistence.

Formatters (fmt_input.rs, fmt_output.rs) consume ToolCatalog/ToolOperation outputs to produce ChatResponseContent elements consumed by the streaming/UI layers; they rely on FileInfo and consistent metadata produced by operations.
notable_relationships: templates/ folder provides prompt templates used by CommandGenerator, GitApp, Compactor summaries, Orchestrator chat contexts and commit message generation — edits to templates change LLM inputs and observed outputs across many flows.

benchmarks/ TypeScript runner is independent but exercises shell commands and environment flows; its outputs and validations are used by CI and local checks and expect consistent command generation and logging semantics.

Infra trait evolution affects cross-crate implementations and test harnesses: changes to EnvironmentInfra or FileReaderInfra must be mirrored in ForgeInfra implementations and in many unit/integration test fixtures.

Public API trait changes (crates/forge_api) ripple to CLI/TUI and any remote/RPC adapters: keep the surface stable or update all callers; conversation persistence and provider auth flows are surfaced here.

CI expectations (protoc, RUSTFLAGS -Dwarnings) and cross-compilation requirements mean edits must preserve build/test hygiene (no new warnings) and may require updating generated code or proto handling.
recommendations: Preserve configuration correctness: when code reads config for provider/model resolution or behavior toggles, obtain it from infra/get_config() or explicitly re-read after update_config to avoid stale behavior.
Respect per-agent provider/model overrides: update resolution sites and any tests to validate per-AgentId selection; ensure suggest/commit-specific config paths are consulted for light-weight flows.
Maintain consistent normalization: tool args must be normalized/stringified and file paths resolved to absolute before persisting or dispatching to services to avoid mismatches and broken transforms.

Keep metrics/truncation invariants: when changing ToolOperation/into_tool_output, ensure conversation Metrics and FileOperation totals use canonical FileInfo (full totals) and temp-file paths for full outputs are preserved.

Align infra trait changes across implementations and tests: if infra signatures change (e.g., read_batch_utf8), update all concrete infra implementations, mocks, and TestContext fixtures to the new contract.
Update tests/fixtures in lockstep with domain changes: run unit/integration tests and update TestContext defaults (parallel_file_reads, FileInfo usage) to prevent regressions.

Run full verification after edits: cargo check/test/workspace, cargo clippy with -D warnings, and the TypeScript benchmark runner (npm scripts) where applicable; ensure protoc and codegen steps executed for crates that depend on generated protos.
